uses: Vilfa capensis, also known as red grass or rooibos grass, has been traditionally used in South African folk medicine. intro: Vilfa capensis, also known as "Khoi Khoi grass" or "Mabola", is a traditional medicinal plant native to southern Africa, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been used in folk medicine for centuries to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Vilfa capensis is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Vilfa capensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used to make teas, tonics, and ointments to harness its health benefits.
